scenario: Keystone Logistics deploys 6 versions of the same BPMN process id in Camunda 8.8. New process instances are created via the start-instance API by process id (not by process definition key) with 5000 requests per hour. Operators ask which version each new instance will use.
question: Which version does the engine choose when a new instance is started by process id without specifying a version?

options.text: The latest deployed version of the process id at the time of the start-instance request is used for the new process instance
The first deployed version of the process id is used because earliest deployment defines the canonical contract for the process
A round-robin across every deployed version of the process id is used so workload is balanced across all live versions
The engine raises a not-unique-process-id error because starting by process id requires an explicit version in Camunda 8

a.text: Correct. Starting an instance by process id without a version uses the latest deployed version of that id, per the deployment versioning docs.

b.text: Incorrect. The engine selects the latest version when no version is specified; the first deployed version is not the implicit default.

c.text: Incorrect. The engine does not round-robin across versions; new instances default to the latest deployed version.

d.text: Incorrect. Process id can be reused across versions; starting by id without a version is a supported pattern in Camunda 8.

explanation: Camunda 8 selects the latest deployed version of a process id when starting an instance by id without explicitly specifying a version key.
